config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang jelas agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat perlindungan hak individu, bukan dengan mengorbankannya begitu saja. Kalau hak pribadi mudah dikalahkan, masyarakat justru berisiko jadi tidak adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse government overreach and pressure on minorities. Public safety and the common good matter, but they should be pursued within firm limits that protect speech, privacy, due process, and equal treatment. If “society” becomes a vague trump card, it can swallow the very rights that keep society legitimate in the first place. To me, the better principle is that individual rights are the default, and collective needs only override them in narrow, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya tidak proporsional, dan yang paling mudah terdampak biasanya kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan pembatasan yang benar-benar perlu, bukan dengan menjadikan hak individu sebagai hal yang bisa dikorbankan kapan saja. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, karena tanpa itu “kepentingan bersama” mudah berubah jadi alat tekanan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am wary of any argument that gives “the public good” broad power to override rights, because that has often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, I do not think rights are absolute in every case, since things like public health, safety, and due process can justify narrow limits when they are clearly necessary and fairly applied. The key difference is that society should have to prove the need for any restriction, not just claim a vague collective interest and expect individuals to give up their protections.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan yang paling sering kena dampaknya justru orang kecil, kelompok minoritas, atau mereka yang tidak punya pengaruh. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat sempit, jelas alasannya, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa paling benar. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi dalih untuk menekan orang yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am very skeptical of any broad “common good” argument, because in practice it has often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach against unpopular people. That said, I do accept limited exceptions for things like public safety, health, and due process, but only when the restriction is narrow, necessary, and clearly justified. If society can override rights too easily, then those rights are not really protecting anyone when they are most needed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
